assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has been declared and a corresponding intragroup receivable/payable has been recognised before consolidation eliminations.
The issue raised is the accounting for foreign currency exposure on that recognised intragroup position, including whether hedge accounting models could be relevant.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 39 identify fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 39 identify cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the non-hedge baseline accounting model for foreign currency monetary items and intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements, including recognition in profit or loss or OCI in specified cases.
